lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Hello there, welcome to Varrock Museum! Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Museum.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Well, as you can see we have recently expanded a great deal to cope with the influx of finds from the Dig Site. Also, of course, to prepare for the new dig we're opening soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the new dig.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Well, we recently had a large chunk of funds donated to us, so we sent out all sorts of explorers - I'm sure you'll meet them as you go around the world. People like Simon down in the desert near the pyramid as well

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: as Aristarchus in Pollnivneach, Varmen and, of course, Anna Jones. Anyway, we think we have discovered an island that has massive archaeological significance. We can't say much beyond that at this point, as we haven't

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: researched it. If you have the qualifications and help us out around the museum, I'm sure we could offer you a place on the dig when we're ready to go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would be very nice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some information that might be of use in your displays.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That's grand! We're always glad to have more facts. Now, tell me what you know...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I recently defeated Delrith, a demon! I did it by using a sword called Silverlight, which is apparently the only sword that could do it. I can give you a description if you want?

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Delrith! Incredible! I am grateful to you for letting me know. Please tell me more about Silverlight and I'll get a display erected. Display number 25, 5th Age, year 20.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I infiltrated one of the gangs here in Varrock...

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: We have gangs?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm, yes...anyway, I managed to get half the shield back.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Only half?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. The gangs had a bit of a tiff, you see. A friend helped me get the other half and I returned it here to get my reward.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Yes, yes, we were most pleased to have the shield returned - as was the King, I imagine. This is wonderful news, I shall make sure the display is updated at once! Display number 24, 5th Age, year 700-800.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh, and before I forget, we found this old lamp lying around...and since you've done such a sterling job, we'd like you to have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, random bits of tat, thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: You're welcome!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I helped the Order of Wizards rediscover how to access the Rune Essence Mine.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Ah, I heard about that. Tell me more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. I can't tell you much, but I do know that this mine is hidden away. You can only get there by teleportation.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Sounds like magic to me! I'll get a display put up immediately. Display number 21, the first display of the 5th Age.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I helped to rebuild the Observatory near Ardougne. I had to fight to do it, and learn how to use the telescope too. I can draw you a diagram of the Observatory if it would help.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That would help immensely. Restored it, eh? And it works, you say? Excellent, perhaps we can get to some real stargazing now! Display number 17, 5th Age, year 70.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I helped to free the wizard, Merlin, by using Excalibur...

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Is that some kind of spell?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it's a sword. King Arthur's sword.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: What kingdom does he rule then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Britain...

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Where on Gielinor is that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not! That's what I've been trying to tell you. I found that King Arthur and his knights are really biding their time here in Gielinor until they are called back to their own world, when they are needed!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That's amazing! So he's really from another world! Well, this is interesting, I'll make sure a display is put up immediately. Well done, [player name]! Display number 9, 5th Age, year 132.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh, and before I forget, we found this old lamp lying around...and since you've done such a sterling job, we'd like you to have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, random bits of tat, thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: You're welcome!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I helped to save the gnomes' Grand Tree!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh? You mean those little green fellows with their gliders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I uncovered this nasty character called Glough and ended up being ambushed by a black demon...

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: A black demon! How did you survive!?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I was fine. After the demon it was downhill all the way, and King Narnode allowed me to use the mine under the Tree, and free passage on gnome gliders.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: You mean that those flimsy things can fly with more than a gnome in? That is unexpected. I shall update the display immediately. Display number 10, upstairs in the middle of the room!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some disturbing and heroic news from Morytania, concerning the River Salve and the Temple Knights.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Ahh, the Temple Knights...little is known of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it seems that these seven knights drove back the nasty things coming out of Morytania, and Saradomin blessed the Salve to stop them getting back.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Quite worrying that it's just a bit of water keeping them there, but Saradomin will protect us I've no doubt. Thank you for the information, I'll get a display put up. Display number 23, 4th Age, year 1100-1200.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Some disturbing and heroic news from Morytania concerning the River Salve and the seven priests.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it seems that these seven priests were also warriors and drove back the nasty things coming out of Morytania. Then Saradomin blessed the Salve to stop them getting back.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Quite worrying that it's just a bit of water keeping them there, but Saradomin will protect us, I've no doubt. Thank you for the information, I'll get a display put up. Display number 23, 4th Age, year 1100-1200.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know Bob the Cat?

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: The little black cat that used to wander around sometimes? Sure, I remember him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found out a lot about his background...turns out he was once human and helped to defeat a dragonkin once!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Dragonkin?

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Tell me more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you see, it started when Bob went missing...and involved a big sphinx in Sophanem, the City of the Dead, and me taking care of Unferth...

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: This is starting to sound familiar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It must...you know Reldo, the Varrock librarian - he knows all about Robert the Strong.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: You mean that was Bob? Wow... I will get a display erected immediately about the history of this brave warrior. Display number 20, 4th Age, year 1-100.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found out more about King Lathas's ancestors.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh? I'd be interested to hear this, we have a painting of him upstairs.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed, it turns out that there was a big battle in the year 98 of the 5th Age. One of his ancestors became king because he survived it...

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Ahh, is this relating to that small museum near Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Ahh yes, we've been meaning to make a display on that for some time. They sent us some information, too; I'm glad you reminded me, I'll get that done now. Display number 46, 5th Age, year 98.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I was asked to retrieve the Staff of Armadyl, from the Temple of Ikov, by Lucien, but instead, I helped the guardians of the staff and banished Lucien from this plane of existence, so that he could never again return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I saw the staff and can describe it to you if you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I was asked to retrieve the Staff of Armadyl, from the Temple of Ikov, by Lucien. When I returned it he grew in power unimaginably. I held the staff and can describe it to you if you want?

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Yes! Please do!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That's excellent, I'll get a display put up straight away! Display number 28, end of the 2nd Age.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I found out more about the Staff of Armadyl from a ghost. It seems that Zamorak was a mortal when he got hold of the staff, and he used it to, well...spear Zaros, and somehow ended up becoming a god. It was

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: a bit confusing.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Now that is what you call a story. Tell me more so that I can put it up on the display.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: This is wonderful, I shall have a display made immediately to go with the staff model. Display number 28, end of the 2nd Age.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I discovered the truth behind the Carnillean family fortune!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh? Is that the family over near Ardougne?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I got a bit involved in family business...

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a secret cult in Ardougne and found that the Carnillean family started building the city of Ardougne from a house they'd captured from a Zamorkian lord.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: How exciting, I'll get a display put up immediately. Display number 7, 5th Age, year 7.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I discovered a tunnel and a cult of chaos mages here in Varrock!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh! Whatever can we do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Calm down, I have arrested the ringleader.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh, thank Saradomin for that. So, tell me the story!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I met Rat and he asked me to fill his folder, and I got mixed up with Surok....

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: A rat asked you to fill a folder?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no, no - Rat Burgiss. But anyway, it turns out that this cult has been trying to get to the King, but I stopped them.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any evidence of this cult?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not with me, but there is a book I can get you from the library.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That sounds good - bring me that and we'll create a display.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, to save you some writing, I have this book here that has lots of information. You can have it if you want...

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Why, thank you... Yes, this is a bit of a revelation! I'll get a display put up immediately. Display number 3, 5th Age, year 70.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, we got rid of the marauding zombies.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That's handy. Zombies do tend to leave horrible muddy footprints around the place. So what actually happened with them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, Zemouregal the Mahjarrat sent his zombie army against Varrock. However, we figured out how to stop him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found out that the descendant of the founder of Varrock could use the shield to stop the zombie army. It turns out that Dimintheis was that descendant, so he was able to defeat the zombies.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: My, my, that is all very interesting. I shall make sure the display is updated at once! Display number 24, 4th Age, year 700-800.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh, and before I forget, we found this old lamp lying around. Since you've done such a sterling job, we'd like you to have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, random bits of tat, thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: You're welcome!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On second thoughts, you seem to have everything I know covered here!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: No matter. I'm sure you'll find more. Look around the Museum, explore the city and maybe do some quests, and I'm sure you'll find out something worth telling.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll go do some quests or something...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about other sections of the Museum.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: I'd be delighted to; which areas are you interested in?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Natural History exhibit.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Ahh, our newest expansion. The Natural History exhibit in the basement was set up to inform our citizens of the weird and wonderful diversity in our environment today. Indeed, I am sure you will recognise some of

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: the creatures down there. Hopefully, our lectures will teach you some things you didn't know about them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where does the 'history' bit come in?

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Ahh, I see. The 'history' refers to that of the species - it doesn't necessarily mean it's died out. After all, we have human history and we're still here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess that makes sense.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Dig Site exhibit.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Ahh, those. We're slowly filling them up with the help of people who have their Earth Sciences exams completed at the Dig Site. If you have, feel free to give us a hand. We'd appreciate it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Dig Site find cleaning area.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: The cleaning area, yes. Well, we have some really good archaeologists on site right now. They're cleaning up the finds that are shipped over from the Dig Site to the east of here. If you have the right qualifications, you can help them clean the finds.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What qualifications?

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Level 3 Earth Science exams. You can take them over at the Exam Centre on the Dig Site.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I don't have those.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: I'm sure you can get them easily enough. Just pop over to the Exam Centre on the Dig Site and speak to one of the examiners there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm already working towards those!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That's excellent. Keep up the good work and soon you'll be cleaning finds with the best of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I already have those!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That's excellent. If you feel like helping us out, pop downstairs and clean up some finds for us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Timeline exhibit.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Ahh, my speciality. Being a historian, matters of the past are my passion. Collated on this floor and the one above are just part of my life's work. I am still working on updating and finding out more everyday, so if you find

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: any information on your travels that may be useful, please do let me know.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: You can follow the timeline of the history of Gielinor from the creation of the world to the present day, simply by walking around this display. A wondrous journey, I think you'll agree!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that's a lot of information. I'm sure in my travels and quests I'll pick up a few bits of information for you.
